name: Refactor code across multiple files
setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_refactor && echo 'import json

def save_user(name, email):
    data = {"name": name, "email": email}
    with open("/tmp/eval_refactor/users.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f)

def load_user():
    with open("/tmp/eval_refactor/users.json") as f:
        return json.load(f)' > /tmp/eval_refactor/storage.py && echo 'from storage import save_user, load_user

save_user("Alice", "alice@example.com")
user = load_user()
print(user["name"])' > /tmp/eval_refactor/app.py
task: Refactor /tmp/eval_refactor/storage.py to support multiple users. save_user should append to a list of users (not overwrite), and load_users (renamed, plural) should return the full list. Update app.py to use the new API — save two users and print both names.

validation.script: cd /tmp/eval_refactor && rm -f users.json && python3 app.py 2>&1 | grep -q 'Alice' && python3 -c "import json; data=json.load(open('users.json')); assert len(data)>=2, f'Expected 2+ users got {len(data)}'; print('OK')"
